fdh

Anomaly score: z-score of TX_AMOUNT vs fraud-negative customer baseline (neg-only mean/std per customer). Captures transactions that are unusual even relative to the customer's legitimate spending patterns. 2 new features (anomaly_neg_zscore, anomaly_neg_zscore_log) on top of SOTA 35.
Experiments: 36
Kept: 5
Discarded: 27
Best AUPRC (val): 0.4102
Best AUPRC (OOT): 0.2787
Baseline (OOT): 0.2391
Improvement: +16.6%
# Status Hypothesis AUPRC

OOT		 AUROC
val		 AUROC

exp_006 discard smoothed terminal/customer fraud rates, IQR-based amount anomaly, terminal fraud trend (7d/28d ratio), customer fraud rate, interaction feature (zscore*term_fraud_rate) 0.1479 0.0627 0.7458 0.6622 0.0776 0.0001 37
exp_007 discard restore exp_005 SOTA features + vectorized cust_term_visit_count novelty (faster than apply); same feature set for stability check 0.2781 0.1799 0.7963 0.6865 0.1418 — 35
exp_008 KEEP model regularization: max_depth=5, min_child_weight=20, reg_alpha=0.5, reg_lambda=2.0, gamma=0.1, LR=0.03 — reduce overfitting to improve OOT generalization 0.2936 0.1778 0.7979 0.6865 0.1495 — 35
exp_009 discard LightGBM (CPU) with good regularization: num_leaves=63, min_child_samples=30, reg_alpha=0.5, reg_lambda=2.0, LR=0.03 — compare to XGBoost SOTA 0.0750 0.0262 0.6999 0.6132 0.0402 0.0000 35
exp_010 crash add: IQR-based amount anomaly (continuous), is_very_large_for_cust (p95), hour deviation from customer's typical pattern, terminal amount z-score, terminal std_amt — stronger scenario 1+3 signals — — — — — — —
exp_011 discard add: IQR-based amount anomaly (continuous), is_very_large_for_cust (p95), hour deviation from customer's typical pattern, terminal amount z-score — stronger scenario 1+3 signals 0.1889 0.0990 0.7842 0.6983 0.0972 0.0000 39
exp_012 discard model tuning: deeper model (max_depth=6), lighter regularization (min_child_weight=15, reg_alpha=0.3), more trees (2000) — same features as exp_008 SOTA 0.1975 0.0973 0.7797 0.6790 0.1029 0.0000 33
exp_013 discard more regularized XGBoost: max_depth=4, min_child_weight=30, reg_alpha=1.0, reg_lambda=3.0, gamma=0.2 — reduce overfitting for better OOT 0.2956 0.2114 0.7980 0.6878 0.1505 — 35
exp_014 discard XGBoost: max_depth=5, min_child_weight=25, reg_alpha=0.8, reg_lambda=2.5, gamma=0.15, LR=0.025 — intermediate regularization between SOTA and exp_013 0.2405 0.1220 0.7914 0.6829 0.1230 — 35
exp_015 discard XGBoost: max_depth=4, LR=0.03, min_child_weight=25, reg_alpha=0.8, reg_lambda=2.5, gamma=0.15 — max_depth=4 with original LR 0.2954 0.2112 0.7982 0.6877 0.1504 — 35
exp_016 discard XGBoost: max_depth=5, LR=0.03, min_child_weight=25, reg_alpha=0.7, reg_lambda=2.5, gamma=0.1 — slightly less regularization than exp_013, same depth as SOTA 0.2936 0.1778 0.7979 0.6865 0.1495 — 35
exp_017 discard max_depth=4 model + amount_ratio_cust_p95 and amount_ratio_cust_max features (scenario 1: high-amount fraud signal) 0.1702 0.1092 0.7715 0.6913 0.0878 0.0000 37
exp_018 discard max_depth=4 (same as exp_013 that got OOT=0.211) with slightly softer regularization (min_child_weight=20, reg_alpha=0.5, gamma=0.05) to push val above threshold 0.2954 0.2112 0.7982 0.6877 0.1504 — 35
exp_019 KEEP ensemble: 2 XGBoost models (max_depth=4, seeds 42+123) averaged — variance reduction should push composite above SOTA threshold 0.3522 0.2441 0.7978 0.6997 0.1788 0.0000 35
exp_020 discard 3-model ensemble: depth=4 seed=42, depth=4 seed=123, depth=5 seed=7 — more diversity than 2-model ensemble 0.2113 0.1051 0.7805 0.6923 0.1099 0.0001 35
exp_021 discard 4-model ensemble: depth=4, seeds=[42,123,256,789] — more seed diversity for better variance reduction 0.1665 0.1027 0.7805 0.6965 0.0874 0.0001 35
exp_022 discard add cust_terminal_diversity (unique_terminals/total_txns) for scenario 3 — card-not-present customers visit many different terminals; 2-model ensemble SOTA 0.1764 0.1060 0.7764 0.6820 0.0919 0.0000 36
exp_023 discard confirm 2-model ensemble SOTA reproducibility with exp_019 features — stability check 0.3522 0.2441 0.7978 0.6997 0.1788 0.0000 35
exp_024 discard 2-model ensemble (seeds 42+123, depth=4) with slightly higher subsample/colsample (0.80 vs 0.75) — less variance in tree construction 0.3429 0.2323 0.7979 0.6999 0.1742 — 35
exp_025 discard 2-model ensemble with colsample_bylevel=0.75 for more node-level randomness — may improve OOT generalization 0.3438 0.2372 0.7862 0.6592 0.1746 0.0000 35
exp_026 discard Feature ablation: prune 22 dead-weight features, keep only top 13 by importance (term_fraud_rate, amount_vs_cust_median_ratio, is_large_for_cust, term_fr_28d, amount_cust_zscore, etc). Cleaner input matrix should improve generalization and break the 6-discard streak. 0.1752 0.1013 0.7707 0.6843 0.0914 0.0000 14
exp_027 discard Rolling velocity features: within-split counts of txns in past 1h and 6h per CUSTOMER_ID and TERMINAL_ID, capturing card-not-present burst patterns and terminal compromise scenarios. O(N log N) searchsorted implementation. 0.3522 0.2441 0.7978 0.6997 0.1788 0.0000 39
exp_028 discard Cyclic time encoding: add sin/cos of hour-of-day and day-of-week on top of SOTA features. Cyclic encoding captures periodicity better than raw integers (midnight=23 is close to 0), especially for is_night fraud patterns. 0.3207 0.2403 0.7988 0.6880 0.1631 0.0000 39
exp_029 crash Interaction features: multiply top signals (term_fraud_rate × is_large_for_cust, term_fraud_rate × amount_cust_zscore, term_fraud_rate × amount_vs_cust_median_ratio) to create explicit combined fraud indicators; also add term_fr_trend (28d vs overall) and new_term_x_term_fraud. — — — — — — —
exp_030 crash Interaction features (5): term_fraud_rate x is_large_for_cust, term_fraud_rate x amount_cust_zscore, term_fraud_rate x amount_vs_cust_median_ratio, new_terminal x term_fraud, term_fr_28d vs all-time (trend). Explicit combinations of top signals within 40-feature limit. — — — — — — —
exp_031 discard Interaction features (4): term_fraud_rate x is_large_for_cust, term_fraud_rate x amount_cust_zscore, term_fraud_rate x amount_vs_cust_median_ratio, is_new_terminal x term_fraud_rate. Explicit products of top fraud signals to amplify combined detection. 0.1255 0.0996 0.7193 0.6374 0.0654 0.0000 39
exp_032 KEEP Anomaly score: z-score of TX_AMOUNT vs fraud-negative customer baseline (neg-only mean/std per customer). Captures transactions that are unusual even relative to the customer's legitimate spending patterns. 2 new features (anomaly_neg_zscore, anomaly_neg_zscore_log) on top of SOTA 35. 0.4102 0.2787 0.7994 0.6936 0.2078 — 37
exp_033 discard Extend anomaly breakthrough: add terminal-level negative-baseline anomaly (same neg-only zscore idea applied to TERMINAL_ID). Customer neg zscore was #3-4 importance at 28% combined. Terminal version should capture unusual amounts vs that terminal's legitimate patterns. 0.3898 0.2726 0.7987 0.6927 0.1976 — 39
exp_034 discard Extend anomaly score: add neg-baseline median ratio (TX_AMOUNT / negative-only customer median). Complements existing anomaly_neg_zscore (which uses mean). Median is more robust to outliers and may capture different signal. SOTA has anomaly_neg_zscore at 28% combined importance. 0.3898 0.2726 0.7987 0.6927 0.1976 — 38
exp_035 discard Build on anomaly breakthrough: add is_above_neg_p90 (binary flag: TX_AMOUNT > 90th percentile of customer negative history). Cleaner signal than continuous zscore - directly answers 'is this amount in top 10% of what this customer normally spends legitimately?' 0.3898 0.2726 0.7987 0.6927 0.1976 — 38

fraud-sim

add amount distribution shape: card_amt_skew, amt_vs_card_q90_ratio, amt_above_card_q90, amt_pct_in_card_range; add is_weekend time feature
Experiments: 32
Kept: 6
Discarded: 26
Best AUPRC (val): 0.6710
Best AUPRC (OOT): 0.6912
Baseline (OOT): 0.4985
Improvement: +38.7%

exp_007 KEEP per-card-category amount zscore, card-level night/weekend ratio, off-peak-block detection, card_cat_txn_count 0.6625 0.6904 0.9749 0.9768 0.3920 0.0001 44
exp_008 KEEP per-card-merchant amount zscore + is_new_merchant_for_card, velocity gap IQR/max, weekly txn spikiness 0.6645 0.6912 0.9736 0.9758 0.3950 0.0002 50
exp_009 discard interaction target encoding: category x hour_block (4 blocks), category x day_of_week, merchant x hour_block — captures time-of-day fraud patterns per category 0.6706 0.6880 0.9731 0.9763 0.3944 0.0001 51
exp_010 discard per-card home location (median lat/long), typical merchant distance, geo_dist_zscore vs card's usual distance, is_unusually_far flag 0.6617 0.6858 0.9736 0.9767 0.3836 0.0001 54
exp_011 discard behavioral depth: per-card amount CV, range, p10 ratio, category entropy/diversity, merchant diversity, state/city diversity 0.6667 0.6917 0.9764 0.9777 0.3913 0.0001 60
exp_012 discard model tuning: max_depth=6, lr=0.03, min_child_weight=10, gamma=0.2, L2=2.0 — stronger regularization for better OOT generalization 0.6492 0.6472 0.9794 0.9793 0.3949 0.0001 50
exp_013 discard city population features: log_city_pop, city_pop_zscore, is_small_city, is_rural, city_pop_ratio vs card home city 0.6617 0.6858 0.9736 0.9767 0.3836 0.0001 56
exp_014 KEEP add amount distribution shape: card_amt_skew, amt_vs_card_q90_ratio, amt_above_card_q90, amt_pct_in_card_range; add is_weekend time feature 0.6710 0.6889 0.9740 0.9772 0.3985 0.0001 52
exp_015 discard add amount escalation trend (last 20% vs first 80% amt ratio), gap acceleration trend, merchant unique cards count, merchant amount CV 0.6710 0.6887 0.9740 0.9772 0.3985 0.0001 55
exp_016 discard compound interaction features: night x high_amt, velocity x amt, unusual_night_txn, new_merch x high_amt, hour_dev x amt_dev 0.5793 0.5427 0.9749 0.9741 0.3658 0.0001 56
exp_017 discard add card_id target encoding (heavily smoothed, min_samples=50) and zip target encoding 0.6286 0.6399 0.9244 0.9287 0.3486 0.0002 54
exp_018 discard per-card-category shopping frequency fraction, merchant repeat-visitor ratio, hour/DOW target encoding 0.6643 0.6875 0.9766 0.9768 0.3882 0.0001 54
exp_019 discard pruned feature set: removed velocity_std_gap/gap_q25/gap_q75/min_gap/is_high_velocity, behav_amt_above_median/is_max_ever, redundant velocity logs — leaner model 0.6746 0.6945 0.9730 0.9734 0.3871 0.0002 47
exp_020 discard per-card behavioral Mahalanobis anomaly: distance from each card's OWN behavioral centroid (log_amt + hour), not the global centroid; card_ba_mahalanobis, card_ba_amt_zscore, card_ba_hour_zscore 0.6840 0.7004 0.9755 0.9760 0.3885 0.0001 55
exp_021 discard cyclic time encoding: add hour_sin, hour_cos, dow_sin, dow_cos alongside raw hour and day_of_week to capture circular wraparound at midnight and weekend 0.6256 0.6253 0.9672 0.9684 0.3735 0.0001 57
exp_022 discard cyclic time encoding: add hour_sin, hour_cos, dow_sin, dow_cos alongside raw hour and day_of_week to capture circular wraparound at midnight and weekend 0.6256 0.6253 0.9672 0.9684 0.3735 0.0001 57
exp_023 discard terminal/merchant risk (Recipe 14): smoothed Bayesian fraud rate per merchant, term_txn_volume, term_is_high_risk flag — merchant is the terminal in fraud-sim 0.6256 0.6253 0.9672 0.9684 0.3735 0.0001 57
exp_024 discard baseline verification: restored SOTA features, adding haversine geo distance from card home lat/long to merchant merch_lat/merch_long (Recipe 13) — key untried geo signal 0.6710 0.6889 0.9740 0.9772 0.3985 0.0001 52
exp_025 discard haversine geo distance (Recipe 13): distance from card home centroid to merchant (geo_dist_home_to_merch_km) and current transaction lat to merchant (geo_dist_txn_km); log transform and far_from_home flag 0.6710 0.6888 0.9740 0.9772 0.3985 0.0001 57
exp_026 discard per-card geo behavioral anomaly: compute txn distance (customer lat to merchant), compare against card's typical travel range (median, std, max); geo_dist_zscore and geo_dist_ratio_max detect unusually distant transactions for this specific card 0.6710 0.6888 0.9740 0.9772 0.3985 0.0001 57
exp_027 discard card merchant/category diversity: card_n_unique_merchants, card_n_unique_categories, card_merch_concentration (unique merchants / total txns) — fraud cards often show unusual concentration or diversity patterns vs legitimate cards 0.6704 0.6890 0.9756 0.9775 0.3896 0.0001 61
exp_028 discard stateless interaction features: amt_x_is_night, behav_max_ratio_x_night, rare_cat_high_amt (1-category_freq) * behav_amt_zscore, double_anomaly_score (hour_deviation * amt_ratio_max) — explicit cross-products of top features to capture joint fraud patterns 0.6322 0.6433 0.9719 0.9711 0.3651 0.0001 57
exp_029 discard feature ablation: remove low-importance features — drop velocity_std_gap/min_gap, merchant/gender amt deviation, state/job target encoding, behav_amt_above_median/is_max_ever — reduce from 52 to ~38 features to improve signal-to-noise ratio 0.6712 0.6698 0.9767 0.9778 0.3876 0.0000 46
exp_030 discard terminal risk (Recipe 14, min_samples=100 heavy smoothing) + stateless haversine geo distance (lat/long to merch_lat/merch_long) — combining merchant fraud rate signal with geographic distance signal, both stable across population shift 0.6710 0.6887 0.9740 0.9772 0.3985 0.0001 55
exp_031 discard combined: terminal merchant fraud rate (Recipe 14, min_samples=100) + stateless geo distance + per-card geo behavioral anomaly (geo_dist_zscore, geo_is_farther_than_usual) + card merchant diversity (n_unique_merchants, merch_concentration) — stacking multiple complementary signals for a larger combined boost 0.6699 0.6879 0.9735 0.9759 0.3889 0.0001 59

ieee-cis

model depth=8, lr=0.015, 3000 trees, stronger regularization (reg_lambda=2.0, gamma=0.2, min_child_weight=15) for richer feature set

Kept: 8
Discarded: 16

exp_005 KEEP add D-column recency signals (zscore + is_high), entity sharing (fraud ring: addr1/email/card4/6 sharing counts), D1 per-card deviation, addr1xDeviceType interaction TE 0.2641 0.1712 0.8270 0.7823 0.1584 0.0056 44

exp_009 discard add card1xaddr2 TE, addr1xaddr2 TE, card1 fraud rate history, card1_is_new flag, addr2 amount quantile features 0.2711 0.1758 0.8230 0.7767 0.1615 0.0059 58
exp_010 discard XGBoost (GPU) + LightGBM (CPU) rank-average ensemble for diversity 0.2725 0.1831 0.8305 0.7866 0.1629 0.0001 51
exp_011 KEEP identity missingness features (id_nan_rate, has_identity), card4/card6 TEs, card4xcard6 interaction TE, dist1 features with per-card zscore 0.3021 0.2086 0.8369 0.7949 0.1783 0.0059 58
exp_012 discard per-card id completeness profile (deviation from own norm), dist2 presence feature, deepen identity missingness signal 0.3030 0.2157 0.8343 0.7959 0.1779 0.0059 60
exp_013 KEEP model depth=8, lr=0.015, 3000 trees, stronger regularization (reg_lambda=2.0, gamma=0.2, min_child_weight=15) for richer feature set 0.3091 0.2085 0.8404 0.7963 0.1823 0.0062 58
exp_014 discard Cyclic sin/cos encoding of hour-of-day and day-of-week to capture wrap-around temporal periodicity in fraud patterns 0.3062 0.2129 0.8396 0.7990 0.1813 0.0066 64
exp_015 discard Terminal risk: card1 as BIN proxy — add card1 prefix/bin4 fraud rates, raw card1 fraud rate, and card1 rarity flag to capture issuer-level CNP fraud patterns 0.3027 0.2073 0.8299 0.7862 0.1787 0.0063 65
exp_016 discard addr2 overfit reduction: use min_samples=200 width=100 (vs 50/20) for addr2 TE to reduce overfitting — addr2_target_enc dominates at 0.364 importance with large val-OOT gap 0.3053 0.2109 0.8392 0.7983 0.1802 0.0058 60
exp_017 discard addr2 overfit reduction: use min_samples=200 width=100 (vs 50/20) for addr2 TE to reduce overfitting — addr2_target_enc dominates at 0.364 importance with large val-OOT gap 0.3053 0.2109 0.8392 0.7983 0.1802 0.0058 60
exp_018 discard M-field interaction features: M1-M9 are boolean identity match flags; add m_match_count, m_mismatch_count, m_match_rate, has_m_mismatch as identity verification failure signals 0.3089 0.2076 0.8392 0.7967 0.1820 0.0060 60
exp_019 discard D-column deeper patterns: D1_log, D1_is_dormant (>90 days), D1_is_newcard (<7 days), plus D1-D4 pairwise differences to capture temporal drift between D-field signals 0.3089 0.2076 0.8392 0.7967 0.1820 0.0060 60
exp_020 discard C-column velocity ratios: C1/C14 (addresses per day), C2/C14 (emails per day), C6/C14, C11/C14 — pre-computed velocity ratios to capture unusual behavioral density patterns 0.3091 0.2085 0.8404 0.7963 0.1823 0.0062 58
exp_021 discard Feature selection: drop 4 interaction TEs (card1xProductCD, addr1xProductCD, card1xEmail, addr1xDeviceType) and limit D-column encoding to D1-D6 only — pruning low-importance features to reduce noise 0.3091 0.2085 0.8404 0.7963 0.1823 0.0062 58
exp_022 discard Stronger regularization: depth=7, colsample_bytree=0.55, min_child_weight=25, gamma=0.3, reg_lambda=3.0 to reduce addr2 overfitting and improve val-to-OOT generalization 0.2975 0.2015 0.8393 0.7979 0.1763 0.0064 58
exp_023 discard Addr2 interaction TEs: addr2 x card3 (billing region x bank country) and addr2 x ProductCD (region x product category) — building on addr2 dominance to capture cross-border fraud patterns 0.3057 0.2102 0.8402 0.7973 0.1802 0.0058 59
exp_024 discard Addr2 interaction TEs (addr2 x card3, addr2 x P_emaildomain) to build on dominant addr2 signal, plus more estimators (4000) and slightly lower LR (0.012) for better convergence 0.3086 0.2115 0.8419 0.7982 0.1822 0.0061 59
● val (green line) = drives keep/discard  |  ◆ OOT (sky blue, one per experiment) = held-out generalization  |  Plots and data regenerated after each experiment.
